records.name: West African Griot Tradition
records.summary: Hereditary praise-singers and oral historians (jali/djeli) of the Mande empires who accompanied narrative song on stringed instruments like the kora and ngoni. Scholars such as Alan Lomax and Paul Oliver noted deep aesthetic parallels — solo voice-and-string performance, melisma, and a singer's ambiguous social status — between griots and early blues singers, though a direct unbroken lineage remains debated rather than documented.
